summary:
The patient has multiple renal angiomyolipomas alongside seizures, which strongly suggests Tuberous Sclerosis Complex as opposed to Von Hippel Lindau disease due to lack of typical VHL associated tumors like hemangioblastomas.

In summary,
the patient’s presentation includes multiple renal angiomyolipomas combined with seizures; these factors point towards an increased likelihood of having Tuberous Sclerosis Complex instead of Von Hippel-Lindau Disease based primarily upon differential diagnostic criteria involving specific tumor types and additional symptomatology such as those related to epilepsy. ``` plaintext Summary:  
A 48-year-old female with seizures, gross hematuria, and kidney lesions suggestive of multiple angiomyolipomas more closely matches the characteristics of Tuberous Sclerosis Complex, particularly when contrasted with conditions like Von Hippel-Lindau Syndrome characterized by different tumoral presentations including brain hemangioblastomas. ```
